description:
You are a flight dynamics engineer designing Trajectory Correction Maneuver 4 (TCM-4) for the Stardust-2 sample return capsule on a hyperbolic Earth-return trajectory. Using the initial Cartesian state and mass properties from ~/Desktop/stardust2_tcm_spec.txt, create a GMAT script to target the Earth Entry Interface (EEI) at 120 km altitude. The script must use a DifferentialCorrector to vary impulsive burn components at the initial epoch and propagate to the 120 km altitude point to achieve a radius of periapsis of 6418.14 km and an inclination of 45.0 deg. Save the script to ~/Documents/missions/tcm4_targeting.script and write the results to ~/GMAT_output/tcm4_targeting_results.txt with exactly these lines:

Achieved_RadPer_km: <value>
Achieved_INC_deg: <value>
Total_Required_DeltaV_mps: <value>

Login: username=ga, password=password123
